results.finalAnswer:
ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/voxel - core gameplay world state lives here, including voxel edits and chunk/simulation interactions
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core - this is the engine orchestration layer where gameplay systems likely hook into lifecycle and shared runtime services
SUBSYSTEM: engine/rendering - gameplay work needs render integration for visible state changes, debugging, and player feedback

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/voxel - gameplay world state, chunk edits, and `WorldBuffer`-driven simulation live here
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core - the main engine loop and shared runtime plumbing connect gameplay systems into the app
SUBSYSTEM: engine/rendering - gameplay changes usually need render-side integration for world feedback and visibility

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/voxel - core gameplay world state, chunk edits, and simulation-facing voxel data live here
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core - engine lifecycle and shared runtime services that gameplay systems plug into
SUBSYSTEM: engine/rendering - gameplay features usually need a render integration path to become visible in-game

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core/Engine_gameplay.cpp - engine-side gameplay loop; owns world ticking, new game/load/save, resets, and the callbacks that bridge engine to game logic
SUBSYSTEM: game/ecs/GameComponents.h - defines the gameplay state model you will edit: health, skills, inventory, magic, status effects, voxel interaction, and AI-facing components
SUBSYSTEM: game/systems/VoxelInteractionSystem.cpp - high-value gameplay system example; touches mining, placing, combat, loot, particles, sounds, and skill XP against the voxel world

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core/Engine_gameplay.cpp - engine-side gameplay loop, state transitions, save/load, and the callback seam into game code
SUBSYSTEM: game/ecs/GameComponents.h - core gameplay state model for player, health, skills, inventory, gear, magic, and status effects
SUBSYSTEM: game/systems/ - concrete ECS gameplay systems (movement, voxel interaction, skills, inventory, death, mobs) registered from `game/main.cpp`

ENTRYPOINT: game/main.cpp
SUBSYSTEM: engine/core/Engine_gameplay.cpp - owns the per-frame Playing loop after startup and is the engine-side bridge into ECS progression, callbacks, save/load, and mob/gameplay ticks
SUBSYSTEM: game/ecs/GameComponents.h - defines the gameplay ECS data model that every player, skill, inventory, status, death, and magic system shares
SUBSYSTEM: game/systems/VoxelInteractionSystem.cpp - best concrete gameplay seam into the voxel world: combat, mining, placement, loot, and skill XP through raycasts and ChunkManager
